SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.adsorber

Class PressureSwingAdsorptionBed

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.process.SimulationBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.ProcessEquipmentBaseClass
neqsim.process.equipment.TwoPortEquipment
neqsim.process.equipment.adsorber.AdsorptionBed
neqsim.process.equipment.adsorber.PressureSwingAdsorptionBed
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, ProcessEquipmentInterface, TwoPortInterface, ProcessElementInterface, SimulationInterface, NamedInterface
public class PressureSwingAdsorptionBed extends AdsorptionBed
Pressure-Swing-Adsorption (PSA) bed pre-configured for hydrogen purification.

Thin specialisation of AdsorptionBed that picks defaults appropriate for industrial H2 purification trains downstream of steam-methane-reforming + water-gas-shift, e.g. the Skarstrom / 4-bed / 6-bed configurations described in Yang (Adsorbents: Fundamentals and Applications, 2003) and Ruthven (Principles of Adsorption and Adsorption Processes, 1984).

Hydrogen has a much lower Langmuir affinity than CO, CO2, CH4, N2 on activated carbon and zeolite 13X, so H2 passes through the bed largely unadsorbed while the heavier components are captured. The bed therefore behaves as an inverted separator: the outlet stream is the H2 product, and the adsorbed components are reported as the tail-gas composition.

Convenience methods:

  • setSorbent(SorbentType) - select a textbook sorbent + bulk density default
  • setRecoveryTarget(double) - cycle-averaged H2 recovery factor (0..1)
  • getH2Purity() - mole fraction H2 in the product stream
  • getH2Recovery() - mole flow H2 in product / mole flow H2 in feed

Only the steady-state, cyclic-steady-state representation is implemented here; cycle-resolved transient simulation is provided by the inherited runTransient method together with AdsorptionCycleController.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim contributors

    • PressureSwingAdsorptionBed

      public PressureSwingAdsorptionBed(String name)
      Construct a PSA bed with the given name and Skarstrom-cycle defaults.
      Parameters:
      name - name of the unit operation

    • PressureSwingAdsorptionBed

      public PressureSwingAdsorptionBed(String name, StreamInterface inletStream)
      Construct a PSA bed with the given name, inlet stream, and Skarstrom-cycle defaults.
      Parameters:
      name - name of the unit operation
      inletStream - feed stream (typically shifted syngas at 20-30 bara)

  • Method Details

    • applyPsaDefaults

      private void applyPsaDefaults()
      Apply typical industrial H2-PSA defaults: Langmuir isotherm, activated carbon, 2 m diameter by 4 m bed height, 35% void fraction, Ergun pressure drop on.

    • setSorbent

      public void setSorbent(PressureSwingAdsorptionBed.SorbentType sorbent)
      Select sorbent type. Updates the adsorbent material identifier and the bulk-density default.
      Parameters:
      sorbent - sorbent selection (activated carbon, zeolite 13X)

    • getSorbent

      public PressureSwingAdsorptionBed.SorbentType getSorbent()
      Get the currently selected sorbent.
      Returns:
      sorbent enum value

    • setRecoveryTarget

      public void setRecoveryTarget(double recoveryTarget)
      Set the cycle-averaged H2 recovery factor. Industrial H2-PSA skids typically achieve 0.75-0.90 depending on the number of pressure-equalisation steps.
      Parameters:
      recoveryTarget - recovery fraction, 0..1

    • getRecoveryTarget

      public double getRecoveryTarget()
      Get the cycle-averaged H2 recovery target.
      Returns:
      recovery fraction, 0..1

    • run

      public void run(UUID id)

      In this method all thermodynamic and unit operations will be calculated in a steady state calculation.

      In steady-state mode the bed is assumed to be at cyclic-steady-state: the outlet composition reflects the equilibrium loading at the feed conditions scaled by an efficiency factor derived from number-of-transfer-units (NTU).

      Steps:

      1. Captures feed H2 mole flow.
      2. Delegates to AdsorptionBed.run(UUID) to perform the equilibrium-NTU calculation, which adsorbs heavier components and lets H2 pass through.
      3. Applies the cycle-averaged recovery factor by venting a fraction of the H2 in the product back into the tail gas (purge / pressure-equalisation losses).
      4. Computes H2 purity, H2 recovery, and the tail-gas mole-flow vector.
      Specified by:
      run in interface SimulationInterface
      Overrides:
      run in class AdsorptionBed
      Parameters:
      id - calculation identifier

    • getH2Purity

      public double getH2Purity()
      Get the H2 mole fraction in the product stream, computed during the last run(UUID).
      Returns:
      hydrogen purity, mole fraction (0..1)

    • getH2Recovery

      public double getH2Recovery()
      Get the H2 recovery (product H2 / feed H2), computed during the last run(UUID).
      Returns:
      hydrogen recovery, dimensionless (0..1)

    • getTailGasMoleFlow

      public double[] getTailGasMoleFlow()
      Get the tail-gas mole flow per component (mol/s), in feed-stream component order. Returns null if run(UUID) has not been called.
      Returns:
      per-component tail-gas mole flow (mol/s), or null

    • getTailGasComposition

      public double[] getTailGasComposition()
      Get the tail-gas composition (mole fractions), in feed-stream component order. Returns null if run(UUID) has not been called or the tail gas is empty.
      Returns:
      per-component tail-gas mole fractions (sum to 1), or null

    • findHydrogenIndex

      private static int findHydrogenIndex(SystemInterface system)
      Find the index of hydrogen in the system component list. Recognises common naming variants.
      Parameters:
      system - thermo system to inspect
      Returns:
      component index of hydrogen, or -1 if not present
